defaultsApplied.status: Past for every event (CSV has no Status column; all CSV events are historical)
defaultsApplied.externalLink: '#' placeholder because the CSV has no external link column
defaultsApplied.featured: Empty string where the CSV Featured cell is blank
defaultsApplied.location: Empty string where the CSV Location cell is blank
defaultsApplied.heroPhoto: Unsplash placeholder URL chosen by category vibe (see Image_Manifest.md for the planned final filenames)

categories.name: AMAs / Founder Chats
categories.description: Intimate sessions where founders unpack hard-won lessons with our network.

events.name: Narrative Violations: The Portkey GTM Story
events.shortDescription: Vrushank Vyas (led GTM at Portkey from first sale to acquisition) on selling AI infra from India to the world.
events.longDescription:
A conversation with Vrushank Vyas, who led GTM at Portkey from the very first sale through the company's acquisition.

Vrushank unpacks how Portkey built its go-to-market motion through what he calls "narrative violations" — and what it actually takes to sell AI infrastructure from India to a global customer base. Portkey is an AI gateway and observability platform that helps companies deploy, manage, and govern LLMs in production.

events.name: AMA with Srinivas Narayanan
events.shortDescription: Intimate dinner with senior tech leaders, hosted by former OpenAI CTO Srinivas Narayanan.
events.featured: Srinivas Narayanan (Former CTO, B2B Applications at OpenAI)
events.longDescription:
An intimate dinner with Srinivas Narayanan, former CTO of B2B Applications at OpenAI, hosted at Together Fund.

The evening was an off-the-record conversation with senior tech leaders from the Together community on the engineering and operating playbook behind shipping enterprise AI products at frontier scale — what actually works, what gets in the way, and where the next 12 months of enterprise AI infrastructure is heading.

events.name: Community Dialogue with OpenAI
events.shortDescription: An evening with the OpenAI team for builders, researchers, and operators working at the frontier of AI systems.
events.longDescription:
An evening with the OpenAI team for builders, researchers, and operators working at the frontier of AI systems.

Not a demo day, not office hours, not a pitch event — a focused conversation on what it actually takes to build AI systems in production. The session opens with a deep-dive presentation from the OpenAI team along with a technical Q&A.

events.name: How We Build and Manage 15,000+ Tools at Composio
events.shortDescription: Jayesh Sharma on how Composio orchestrates 15,000+ tools that AI agents call in production. Part of the AI Founders: Early Playbook series.
events.longDescription:
Jayesh from the Composio team on how AI agents are used to discover, build, test, and maintain 15,000+ tools across 900+ applications — entirely end-to-end and largely without human intervention.

How do you manage thousands of integrations across hundreds of apps without drowning in manual work? The session unpacks how agent-driven systems run real integration workflows in production. Aimed at founders building agentic systems and operators integrating them.

events.name: Fireside with the OpenAI Codex Team
events.shortDescription: The OpenAI Codex team walks through the new Codex, computer-use agents, and a live demo of voice cloning under 15 seconds.
events.longDescription:
A closed-door session with the OpenAI Codex team for founders and senior engineers in the Together community.

The team walked the room through the redesigned Codex, the new computer-use agents launch, and demonstrated a speech-to-speech model that cloned a voice in under 15 seconds across live use cases. Q&A ran long; attendees left with a sharp view of where the next wave of agent infrastructure is heading.

events.name: The Composio Blueprint: Inside the Mind of an AI Infra Founder
events.shortDescription: Soham Ganatra and Manav Garg on building AI infrastructure that 50+ startups now depend on. Part of the AI Founders: Early Playbook series.
events.longDescription:
Soham Ganatra (co-founder of Composio) in conversation with Manav Garg at Samarthanam Auditorium.

How Composio went from an early bet on agentic tooling to becoming the infrastructure that dozens of AI startups now depend on. Soham unpacked the early product calls, the wedges that worked, and the wedges that didn't. Part of the AI Founders: Early Playbook series — a long-running line of intimate sessions where founders share what they actually learned.

events.name: Emergent: Journey to $10M ARR in 2 Months
events.shortDescription: Mukund Jha (Emergent CEO) and Manav Garg on scaling a global agentic product from India to $10M ARR in two months.
events.featured: Mukund Jha (Founder & CEO, Emergent) with Manav Garg
events.longDescription:
A fireside with Mukund Jha (founder & CEO of Emergent) and Manav Garg at Bangalore International Centre.

Two months. $10M ARR. Globally distributed users. Mukund walked the room through the product decisions, the GTM motions, and the team shape that made it possible — and the things that broke along the way. A candid look at what it takes to ship a global agentic product out of India and have it land.

events.name: Google: AI, Search & Beyond - Fireside Chat with Srinivasan V (VP Search, Google)
events.shortDescription: Fireside chat with Srinivasan V (VP Search, Google) on the future of AI, search, and what comes next.
events.longDescription:
A fireside chat with Srinivasan V (VP Search, Google), hosted in Burlingame, California.

A conversation on where AI is taking search — what changes under the hood, what changes for end users, and what changes for the businesses built on top of search distribution. Co-hosted with Insight Partners for the Bay Area AI community.

events.name: Fireside chat with Neha Singh (Co-founder of Tracxn): Masterclass on navigating the entrepreneurial ladder
events.shortDescription: A masterclass with Tracxn co-founder Neha Singh on navigating the entrepreneurial ladder as a woman founder.
events.longDescription:
A masterclass with Neha Singh, co-founder of Tracxn, hosted at Together Fund in Bengaluru.

Two parts: the founder journey of building Tracxn into a category-defining data platform, and a frank look at navigating the entrepreneurial ladder as a woman founder in Indian tech. Q&A from the room turned the session into a working discussion on the real dilemmas attendees were sitting with.

events.name: Monetizing AI w/ Madhavan & Girish
events.shortDescription: Madhavan and Girish Mathrubootham on how AI-native startups should think about monetization. Sideline session at SaaSBoomi.
events.longDescription:
A sideline session at SaaSBoomi with Madhavan and Girish Mathrubootham.

The thorniest open question for most AI-native startups right now: how do you price something whose marginal cost moves every week? Madhavan and Girish stress-tested several models live with the room — usage-based, outcome-based, hybrid — and traded notes on what's actually sticking with customers and what isn't.

categories.name: Hackathons
categories.description: Two-day sprints where builders ship something real, together.

events.name: Claw Hack BLR
events.shortDescription: Bangalore edition of the Claw Hack — agentic-AI builders shipping working products in 24 hours.
events.featured: Co-hosted with Zo World, Boldrin, Colin Nebius
events.longDescription:
A one-day build sprint for AI and deep-tech builders in Bengaluru.

The format is straightforward: show up, pick a problem, build an AI agent, ship it by end of day. No decks, no spectators — builders in a room with laptops, APIs, credits, and a shared obsession with making things work. Aimed at founders, engineers, and operators who'd rather ship than talk about shipping.

events.name: OpenClaw Hackathon
events.shortDescription: All-night build sprint with the Zo World community — agents, voice, and payments stacks shipped end-to-end.
events.longDescription:
An all-night build sprint with the Zo World community.

Builders shipped end-to-end through agents, voice, and payments stacks in a single sitting. Demos ran late into the night, with the strongest teams pitching live to the Together partners at sunrise.

events.name: Runtogether Hackathon
events.shortDescription: 10-hour in-person execution sprint in Bangalore. Idea to working demo to investor pitch deck in one day; top teams fast-tracked to SwarmSpace.
events.longDescription:
A 10-hour in-person execution sprint in Bangalore. Upcoming on January 31, 2026.

Solo founders and small teams (2-3) go from an idea to a working product demo to an investor pitch deck in a single day, using runable.com. The strongest teams pitch live to Together Fund partners; winners earn a fast-track to SwarmSpace (up to $1M funding opportunity), Runable subscriptions, and more. Partners on the day: Anthropic (LLM), Eleven Labs (voice AI), Stripe (payments).

Aimed at people who ship, not just ideate — solo founders, early-stage teams, engineers and designers who build end-to-end, and students serious about startups.

events.name: Together: Voice AI Hackathon
events.shortDescription: Two-day flagship hackathon: where conversation meets code. Builders stress-test the new wave of voice agents alongside Together portfolio engineers.
events.longDescription:
A 48-hour Voice AI Hackathon at the Together Fund office — a build sprint to prototype the next generation of voice-first experiences.

Whether you were building AI voice agents for customer support, real-time speech-to-action workflows, audio-native games and creative tools, or just tinkering with what becomes possible when voice meets AI — the brief was the same: where conversation meets code. Half the room was Together portfolio engineers; half was the wider Bangalore community.

categories.name: Community Meetups
categories.description: Recurring gatherings - mixers, demo nights, pickleball - where the Together network meets in person.

events.name: AI Mixer: Together x Google Cloud
events.shortDescription: Chennai AI mixer for founders and operators building on Google Cloud.
events.longDescription:
An AI social mixer and panel discussion on GTM engineering, held in Chennai.

Not a conference, not a pitch night, not a business-card networking event — a focused, high-signal gathering for people serious about where AI is heading. The panel brings together operators who've scaled AI products and teams from the inside, in conversation with builders, operators, and leaders working at the frontier of AI.

events.name: Together Fund's AI Mixer
events.shortDescription: AI mixer for founders and operators in the Together community.
events.longDescription:
An evening at Together Fund for founders, researchers, operators, and investors building at the frontier of AI.

Curated room across foundation models and infrastructure, memory and agent architectures, robotics and embodied AI, applications across enterprise and consumer, and deep-tech (semiconductors, materials, data infra). No panel, no slides — focused conversation between people building real things.

events.name: Pickleball with Together
events.shortDescription: Recurring pickleball mornings — Together founders and operators on the courts.
events.longDescription:
What happens when builders, operators, and investors swap boardrooms for a pickleball court?

An easygoing evening of pickleball plus meet-and-greet with the Together Fund team and a curated group of operators, founders, and ecosystem friends. Not a panel, not a pitch event — good games, good conversations, great people. Held at GoRally Pickleball Indiranagar.

events.name: Shippin' Hours (@hsrhackerhouse)
events.shortDescription: Recurring coworking + demo nights at HSR Hacker House — show what you've shipped, get sharp feedback.
events.longDescription:
A recurring numbered coworking + demo session at HSR Hacker House. Two parts:

Deep work — 3 to 3.5 hours of silent work on your individual builds or projects alongside others doing the same. Not just for software engineers; bring whatever you want to push forward — design, portfolio, startup ideas, product features.

Demos — 30 minutes at the end to share what you got done, brainstorm together, and trade questions.

events.name: Together Fridays: Beer (on us!) and You!
events.shortDescription: Casual end-of-week drinks for the Together community at Brau Galie, Bangalore.
events.longDescription:
Casual end-of-week drinks for the Together community at Brau Galie Micro Brewery & Pub in Bangalore.

An open invite — drop in after work, run into the network, leave with a new collaborator. No agenda, no slides, no pitches.

events.name: Thursday To-Do: Sing, Sip, Repeat
events.shortDescription: Karaoke and drinks at The Biere Club HSR — a Together community night out.
events.longDescription:
A Together community night out at The Biere Club, HSR Layout.

Karaoke and drinks. The kind of off-screen energy that the rest of the working week doesn't leave room for, with the people you're already running into in workshops, hackathons, and demo days.

events.name: Together Consumer AI Demo Day
events.shortDescription: Portfolio + community demo day for founders shipping consumer-AI products.
events.longDescription:
A portfolio + community demo day for founders shipping consumer-AI products, hosted at Together Fund.

AI is spawning consumer product categories that didn't exist 18 months ago. The Demo Day brought the Together community and adjacent builders together for an evening of working demos, structured feedback, and post-demo introductions.

events.name: Together AGM Networking Dinner
events.shortDescription: Closing dinner of Together's Inaugural AGM at The Leela Palace, Bangalore.
events.longDescription:
The closing dinner of Together's Inaugural Annual General Meeting, held at The Leela Palace, Bangalore.

After the day's structured AGM sessions, a smaller group of LPs and senior portfolio founders moved to dinner for an unstructured continuation of the day's conversations — across investment thesis, portfolio progression, and the next stretch of fund activity.

events.name: Together's Inaugural Annual General Meeting (AGM)
events.shortDescription: Together's first Annual General Meeting with LPs and portfolio founders at The Leela Palace.
events.longDescription:
Together's first formal Annual General Meeting, held at The Leela Palace, Bangalore.

A working AGM, not a ceremonial one. The fund's first formal gathering of LPs and portfolio founders to share what's worked across the investment thesis, where the active investing focus is heading, and what the next stretch of fund activity looks like.

events.name: Together AI Summit '24
events.location: The Leela Palace Bengaluru - Garden City's Only Modern Palace Hotel
events.shortDescription: Together's annual flagship summit for AI founders, operators, and researchers — keynotes, panels, dinner.
events.longDescription:
Together's annual flagship summit for AI founders, operators, and researchers, held at The Leela Palace, Bangalore in December 2024.

A full evening of keynotes from founding partners, panels with portfolio and ecosystem leaders, startup showcases from across the India AI build-out, and dinner. The 2024 edition focused on the operating playbook for AI-native companies in the wake of the foundation-model boom — what changes in product, in GTM, and in team-shape.

events.name: Get Together '24! 🎉
events.location: The Leela Palace Bengaluru - Garden City's Only Modern Palace Hotel
events.shortDescription: Year-end celebration of India AI & SaaS with the Together community at The Leela Palace.
events.longDescription:
A year-end community celebration at The Leela Palace, Bangalore.

Less program, more people. An evening to mark the year in India AI & SaaS with the Together community — founders, operators, partners, and the team in one room before the year wrapped.

events.name: HealthCare AI Mixer by Together Fund
events.location: PHD Terrace at Dream Midtown, New York
events.shortDescription: Healthcare-AI founders and operators exchange playbooks at a rooftop mixer in NYC.
events.longDescription:
A rooftop mixer for healthcare-AI founders and operators at PHD Terrace, Dream Midtown in New York.

Healthcare-AI is one of the categories where regulation, distribution, and model choice all interact in unusual ways. The mixer brought together builders and investors active in that space for an evening of unstructured conversation across the active questions in the field.

events.name: Mumbai Fintech Happy Hour | 28th Aug, 2024
events.shortDescription: Mumbai fintech community mixer for founders, operators, and investors.
events.featured: Co-hosted with This Week in Fintech
events.longDescription:
A Mumbai fintech community mixer for founders, operators, and investors.

An open-floor evening across infrastructure, regulation, lending, payments, and the early-stage product surface. The format leaves the conversations to be set by whoever shows up — what's most interesting tends to surface that way.

events.name: India SaaS & AI Opportunity by Insight Partners & Together Fund
events.shortDescription: Bay Area mixer celebrating the rise of India SaaS and the AI opportunity ahead.
events.longDescription:
A Bay Area mixer celebrating the rise of India SaaS, held in Burlingame, California.

An evening of conversation on the next decade of India SaaS, with focus on where AI changes both the product surface and the GTM motion. Aimed at SaaS and AI founders, operators, and investors who run both sides of the US-India corridor.

events.name: SaaS & AI Opportunity in the US - India corridor by Flybridge Capital Partners & Together Fund
events.shortDescription: NYC mixer on the SaaS x AI opportunity across the US–India corridor.
events.longDescription:
An evening mixer at Flybridge Capital Partners' New York office, in partnership with Together Fund.

How does a startup whose engineering happens in India but whose customers are in the US set up its first 18 months? The mixer turned that question over with a few dozen founders and operators working that corridor today — across product, sales, and the inevitable timezone arithmetic.

events.name: AI Mixer with Together Fund
events.shortDescription: Together Fund mixer for AI founders and operators — early in our 15+ Generative AI investments arc.
events.longDescription:
An evening mixer for AI founders and operators in the Together community, early in our 15+ Generative AI investments arc.

Topics ranged across foundation-model choice, agent reliability, and the GTM motion for technical AI products. The mixer set the tone for many of the recurring formats that followed — workshops, fireside chats, and Summit later in the year.

events.name: Together's AI Summit: CoPilot
events.location: The Leela Palace Bengaluru - Garden City's Only Modern Palace Hotel
events.shortDescription: Together's first AI Summit — keynote by founding partners plus product demos from leading AI-first startups.
events.longDescription:
Together's first AI Summit — CoPilot — held at The Leela Palace, Bangalore in December 2023.

Keynote by founding partners Girish Mathrubootham and Manav Garg on the emerging opportunities in AI, followed by product showcases from leading AI-first startups across the India ecosystem. The inaugural edition of what's since become the fund's annual flagship event.

categories.name: Podcasts + Workshops
categories.description: Live recordings and deep-dive talks from operators, researchers, and founders.

events.name: From Human Video to Robotic Action - How to Build General-Purpose Robots
events.shortDescription: Talk on learning robotic policies from raw human video — the path to building general-purpose robots.
events.longDescription:
The 4th session of our monthly research salon — an intimate, research-driven discussion on the next frontier of embodied AI.

How do we transfer skills from abundant human videos to real robotic execution without drowning in noisy visual data? Inspired by recent work on Contrastive Latent Action Pretraining (CLAP) and dual-formulation VLA systems, the session explores a new approach to general-purpose robotics from people actively building in the space.

events.name: Beyond Attention - Memory, Surprise, and Continual Learning in Modern AI
events.shortDescription: Reading-circle deep-dive into post-attention architectures and continual learning in modern AI.
events.longDescription:
A discussion circle on the next frontier of sequence modeling — how AI systems can learn, remember, and adapt in real time without forgetting.

Drawing on recent breakthroughs including Titans, MIRAS, and Nested Learning, the session works through a unifying view of memory, optimization, and architecture — moving beyond static context windows toward systems that actually adapt as they encounter new data.

events.name: How to Build a Conscious Machine?
events.shortDescription: Research talk on what it would take to build a conscious machine, and what we still don't know.
events.longDescription:
A discussion circle hosted at Lossfunk on one of the most profound questions in science and philosophy: what is consciousness, and can we build it?

The session draws from a recent paper that formalizes an infinite stack of abstraction layers to describe all possible worlds, and unpacks what that means for building systems that experience anything at all. An evening for people who want to sit with hard questions rather than dodge them.

events.name: Building with Agents: Inside Modern AI Orchestration⚡️Powered by AWS
events.shortDescription: Online deep-dive into multi-agent orchestration patterns — retrievers, planners, executors. Powered by AWS.
events.longDescription:
An online deep-dive into multi-agent orchestration patterns, powered by AWS.

As AI systems evolve, single-model pipelines are giving way to multi-agent systems — retrievers, planners, executors all working as a coordinated whole. The session covered the patterns that are working in production today and the rough edges that aren't yet solved.

events.name: Research Paper Reading Circle: Oak Architecture by Richard Sutton
events.shortDescription: Reading circle on Richard Sutton's Oak architecture — discussion + Q&A on lifelong reinforcement learning.
events.longDescription:
An intimate evening of close reading and discussion built around Richard Sutton's Oak Architecture paper.

An open space for curious minds — builders, researchers, enthusiasts — to dig into cutting-edge research together and trade perspectives section by section. Pre-reading is required so the conversation can move fast and stay specific.

events.name: Dropout. Three Pivots. Scaling Fast: The Metaforms Story
events.shortDescription: Akshat Tyagi (Metaforms CEO) and Manav Garg on going from dropout to fast-scaling AI company in 18 months.
events.featured: Akshat Tyagi (Co-founder & CEO, Metaforms) with Manav Garg
events.longDescription:
A live podcast recording at Bangalore International Centre with Akshat Tyagi (co-founder & CEO of Metaforms) and Manav Garg.

Dropout. Three pivots. From scratch to fast-scaling AI company in 18 months. Akshat walked through the founder calls that turned each pivot, the customer signals that drove them, and what the next eighteen months look like.

events.name: Imagination and Composition: Building the next generation of AI reasoning systems
events.shortDescription: Amit Sharma (Microsoft Research) on pushing LLMs from pattern recognition into composable reasoning.
events.longDescription:
Amit Sharma, principal researcher at Microsoft Research, on moving large language models past surface-level pattern matching.

What does it take to get an LLM to handle long chains of reasoning, hypothetical scenarios, and composition of intermediate steps without falling apart? Amit walked the room through the current methods, where they break, and the research lines pointing at more robust reasoning systems.

events.name: Scaling LLMs: Parameters or Thinking or Data?
events.shortDescription: Prateek Jain (Google DeepMind) on what actually scales modern LLMs — model size, test-time thinking, or data.
events.featured: Prateek Jain (Principal Scientist and Director, Google DeepMind)
events.longDescription:
Prateek Jain — Principal Scientist & Director at Google DeepMind and Research Lead on Gemini Model Design — on the science and engineering behind scaling LLM quality.

How do you truly scale model intelligence: by adding parameters, giving them more thinking time at inference, or exposing them to richer task-specific data? Prateek walks through the empirical evidence across each lever and makes the case for where the next round of gains is most likely to come from.

events.name: Data management for the AI era
events.shortDescription: Ashwin Rajeeva (Acceldata) on automating data pipelines and future-proofing infrastructure for the AI era.
events.longDescription:
Ashwin Rajeeva, co-founder of Acceldata, on data infrastructure for the AI era — held at Together Fund.

What does a data stack look like when AI workflows are first-class consumers, not bolt-ons? Ashwin walked the room through the choices that pay off in production — observability across pipelines, controls around data quality, and the architectural decisions that hold up when models start writing back into the system.

events.name: Inside Your Digital DNA: A Talk on User Embeddings & Truly Personal AI
events.shortDescription: Drew Kirchhoff (ShareD) on how user embeddings power truly personal AI assistants.
events.longDescription:
Drew Kirchhoff, founder of ShareD, on user embeddings as the substrate for personal AI.

How does a compact numerical representation of who you are — what you read, who you talk to, what you care about — become useful input to an AI assistant making decisions on your behalf? Drew walked the room through how embeddings get constructed in practice, where the product surface is interesting, and the privacy questions that arrive immediately.

events.name: From Imitation to Intelligence: Lessons from Autonomous Driving to Humanoids
events.shortDescription: Srikanth Vidapanakal (xSpecies AI) on porting autonomous-driving lessons into general-purpose robotics.
events.longDescription:
Srikanth Vidapanakal, founder & CEO of xSpecies AI, on porting autonomous-driving lessons into general-purpose robotics.

A decade of autonomous-driving research yielded a playbook for learning policies from massive data. Humanoid robotics is now trying to port that playbook into a new domain — walking, manipulation, reasoning about the physical world. The talk covered what transfers, what doesn't, and what comes next.

events.name: Podcast with Composio
events.shortDescription: Live podcast recording at Bangalore International Centre with the Composio founders on building AI infrastructure.
events.longDescription:
A live podcast recording at Bangalore International Centre with the Composio founders.

A long-form conversation on what it took to build AI infrastructure that other AI startups now depend on — founder narrative on the early bets, the engineering choices that scaled, and what's changed in the year since the first thousand customers.

events.name: Podcast with Emergent
events.shortDescription: Live podcast recording at Bangalore International Centre with the Emergent founders on scaling agentic products.
events.longDescription:
A live podcast recording at Bangalore International Centre with the Emergent founders.

How Emergent went from idea to global agentic product — founder narrative on the product calls, the team shape, and the GTM motion that drove the early scaling arc. The conversation that set up many of the later sessions on the same topic.

featuredEvents.displayDescription: Intimate dinner with senior tech leaders, hosted by former OpenAI CTO Srinivas Narayanan.

featuredEvents.displayName: Emergent: Journey to $10M ARR in 2 Months
featuredEvents.displayDescription: Mukund Jha (Emergent CEO) and Manav Garg on scaling a global agentic product from India to $10M ARR in just two months.
Our annual flagship — keynotes, panels, dinner. Editions in 2023 (CoPilot) and 2024 (Summit '24).
A masterclass with Tracxn co-founder Neha Singh on navigating the entrepreneurial ladder as a woman founder.
